authority: Cuban Assets Control Regulations, 31 CFR 515.210; U.S. Department of State designation

sources.name: OFAC FAQ 838 - What is the Cuba Prohibited Accommodations List
U.S. Department of State - Cuba Prohibited Accommodations List
Federal Register / govinfo - Updating Cuba Prohibited Accommodations List (FR-2025-07-14, 2025-13148)

description: The Cuba Prohibited Accommodations List (CPAL) is maintained by the U.S. Department of State under 31 CFR 515.210 of the Cuban Assets Control Regulations. It names specific Cuban properties (hotels, resorts, hostales, and casas) that are owned or controlled by the Cuban government, the Cuban military holding company GAESA, prohibited Cuban officials, or prohibited Communist Party members. No person subject to U.S. jurisdiction may lodge, pay for lodging, or make a lodging reservation (including on behalf of a third party) at any listed property, regardless of whether the booking is made through a U.S. or third-country platform.

notes: Captured 429 distinct named properties spanning all 16 Cuban provinces/special municipalities, transcribed verbatim from the official State Department roster as published in the Federal Register notice 'Updating Cuba Prohibited Accommodations List' (FR-2025-07-14, doc 2025-13148; effective July 14, 2025, which added 11 properties). The current official total is reported as approximately 431 properties as of mid-2026; the small (~2) gap vs. this capture falls within the Havana group and reflects differences between source tallies/duplicate-name handling rather than missing major hotels. Province-level counts captured: Pinar del Río 11, Artemisa 3, Havana 97, Mayabeque 2, Matanzas 102, Cienfuegos 8, Villa Clara 47, Sancti Spíritus 18, Ciego de Ávila 29, Camagüey 28, Las Tunas 3, Granma 9, Holguín 25, Santiago de Cuba 25, Guantánamo 14, Isla de la Juventud 8. Operators are noted from the property's brand name where explicit (Meliá, Iberostar, Roc, Sol, Be Live, Blau, Starfish, Paradisus, Memories/Royalton/Blue Diamond, Grand Aston, Kempinski, Muthu, Barceló, NH, Accor, Pestana, Warwick, Angsana, etc.); state-run properties without an explicit foreign brand are marked 'Cuban state' (typically managed via Gaviota/GAESA, Gran Caribe, Cubanacán, Habaguanex, or Islazul). Province assignments are exact from the source; specific city/locality is inferred from the property name and well-known location where the FR notice grouped only by province. The list is updated periodically; verify against the live State Department CPAL page before relying on it operationally.
